statement: For every S in C* and every machinery-closed admissible representation R satisfying P*, there exists an effective, registered-query-total, equivalence-stable domain of recoverable reasoning commitments with distinct failure and Unknown outcomes.
formal_shape: forall S in C*, forall R in E*: P*(S,R) and Closed(R) -> exists K_R RecoverableCommitmentDomain(K_R,S,R)
proof_strategy: extract commitment-denoting answers from the registered faithful query interface
quotient only by the frozen bidirectional commitment-equivalence relation
use machinery closure to charge every extraction procedure to the representation package
use semantic, information, query-totality, and error/Unknown fidelity clauses of P* to establish effectiveness and totality
construct a contradiction witness whenever a faithful query lacks a recoverable commitment referent
separating_countermodels: same final output but two incompatible internal commitments
query answers produced by a concealed decoder
commitment identity changes under a permitted renaming
failed recovery silently encoded as Unknown
partial query interface omitting a registered commitment question
